rows.notes: Central page title, clearly written and centered at top of page. No monetary amounts in this line.
Description legible. Amount columns aligned to right-hand ruling: reads '5 10 -' so 5 pounds, 10 shillings, 0 pence. Column boundaries unambiguous.
rows.english_description: Rent received from Little Wittenham (reference P.T.D. 16).
rows.english_description_with_amount: Rent received from Little Wittenham (reference P.T.D. 16), amounting to £5 10s 0d.

rows.description: of Mr Pigaud Mr Whitreds Plate money
rows.notes: Amount clearly '10 - -' (10 pounds). Description handwriting is less certain (spelling of names ambiguous); chosen reading 'Pigaud' / 'Whitreds' based on visible letter shapes but there are multiple plausible readings of the names.
rows.english_description: Plate money (fee toward college plate) received relating to Mr Whitred, paid by Mr Pigaud.
rows.english_description_with_amount: Plate money relating to Mr Whitred, paid by Mr Pigaud, amounting to £10 0s 0d.

rows.notes: Amount reads '2 13 - 3/4' in columns: pounds 2, shillings 13, pence column blank (interpreted as 0), fraction '3/4' clearly visible after the dash — recorded as 0.75. Description mostly legible though surname uncertain (read as 'Steyes'); punctuation (hyphen) present before 'Bursans'.
rows.english_description: Increment payment for the bursar received from Mr Steyes.
rows.english_description_with_amount: Increment payment for the bursar received from Mr Steyes, amounting to £2 13s 0¾d.

rows.description: Settled with Mr Hartlin for Weston
rows.notes: No monetary figures appear on this line; clearly a narrative/settlement entry. Handwriting for names and place ('Mr Hartlin' and 'Weston') is legible.
rows.english_description: Account settled with Mr Hartlin in respect of Weston.
rows.english_description_with_amount: Account settled with Mr Hartlin in respect of Weston (amount not stated).

rows.description: viz Money Rent to Mich mas last as usual
rows.notes: Continuation/clarification line (no amounts in the pounds/shillings/pence columns). 'viz Money Rent to Mich mas last as usual' is readable though spacing/abbreviation 'Mich mas' interpreted as Michaelmas.
rows.english_description: Money rent received at Michaelmas as usual.
rows.english_description_with_amount: Money rent received at Michaelmas as usual (amount not stated).

rows.notes: The isolated numeral '265' appears centered/inline with the description block rather than in the rightmost amount columns. Treated as descriptive/note text (no aligned £ s d), reading of the digits is clear but purpose ambiguous.
rows.english_description: A receipt or ledger figure recorded as 265 (unclear whether this denotes pounds or a reference).

rows.notes: Description 'Corn Rent' clear. Amount aligned to columns reads '10.4.10' → £10 4s 10d. Column alignment and digits are unambiguous.
Standalone numeric line in the amount columns '275.4.10' — clearly a subtotal/total. Pounds 275, shillings 4, pence 10. Alignment and digits clear.
Label 'Land Tax Ded.' clear. Amount reads '34.9 -' aligned to columns → £34 9s 0d. No ambiguity in columns or digits.
Numeric subtotal line '240.17.10' in amount columns. Matches arithmetic (275.4.10 - 34.9.0 = 240.17.10), confirming alignment and reading.
Label 'Prof: Tax' clear. Amount reads '24.1.6' in columns → £24 1s 6d. Clear digits and alignment.
rows.english_description: Tax or rate labelled 'Prof: Tax' paid (deducted from receipts).
rows.english_description_with_amount: Tax labelled 'Prof: Tax' paid, amounting to £24 1s 6d.

rows.notes: Total line 'Received clear' with amount '216.16.4' aligned to columns. Arithmetic consistent with previous subtotal less Prof. Tax; numbers and columns are clear.

rows.description: of Mr Crowley Dr Shortridges Benefaction due at Michaelmas
rows.notes: Amount reads clearly as '99 - -' (£99). Description is partially legible but personal names/orthography are uncertain ('Crowley' and 'Shortridge' are best-fit readings from flourished script); parenthetical note following this line in manuscript is faint and not fully transcribed.
rows.english_description: Benefaction (legacy or gift) for Dr Shortridge due at Michaelmas, received from Mr Crowley.
rows.english_description_with_amount: Benefaction for Dr Shortridge due at Michaelmas, received from Mr Crowley, amounting to £99 0s 0d.

rows.description: of the Room of Dr Richards rent not accounted for by the Rector last year
rows.notes: Amount columns read '162.6.' → interpreted as £162 6s 0d. The running description is somewhat crowded and a few words ('accounted') required interpretation from context; numeric columns are clear.
rows.english_description: Rent for the room of Dr Richards which was not accounted for by the Rector in the previous year (arrears) received.
rows.english_description_with_amount: Rent for the room of Dr Richards not accounted for by the Rector in the previous year, amounting to £162 6s 0d.

rows.description: of Mr Rosdewe Dean, library money
rows.notes: '20 - -' in the amount columns is clear (£20). The name/surname in the description is faint and ambiguous ('Rosdewe' is the best reading); the phrase 'Dean, library money' is readable.
rows.english_description: Library money received from Mr Rosdewe, the Dean.
rows.english_description_with_amount: Library money received from Mr Rosdewe, the Dean, amounting to £20 0s 0d.

rows.description: of Do. Deans Fees for the House
rows.notes: Amount reads '17.13.4' clearly in the £ s d columns. Description 'Do. Deans Fees for the House' (i.e. 'Ditto' / Dean's fees) is reasonably legible; digits are unambiguous.
rows.english_description: Dean's fees for the house received from the Dean (same payer as previous entry).
rows.english_description_with_amount: Dean's fees for the house received from the Dean, amounting to £17 13s 4d.

rows.description: of Mr Purdue for an old Door & Casement
rows.notes: Amount reads '3.3.-' → £3 3s 0d. Description (Mr Purdue, old door & casement) is fairly clear in the handwriting.
rows.english_description: Payment received from Mr Purdue for an old door and casement (sale of materials).
rows.english_description_with_amount: Payment received from Mr Purdue for an old door and casement, amounting to £3 3s 0d.

rows.description: of Mr Gillabb Bursans Increment at Mich
rows.notes: Amount '177.19.-' clearly visible (£177 19s 0d). The name in the description is uncertain in several letters ('Gillabb' is a best-effort reading); 'Bursans Increment at Mich' (Bursar's increment at Michaelmas) inferred from context and partial legibility.
rows.english_description: Bursar's increment at Michaelmas received from Mr Gillabb.
rows.english_description_with_amount: Bursar's increment at Michaelmas received from Mr Gillabb, amounting to £177 19s 0d.

rows.description: of the Room of Dr Richards increment of Michas summer 1807 paid by Mr Glubb & not accounted for
rows.notes: Amount reads '88.3.6 1/2' in the amount columns: pounds 88, shillings 3, pence 6 and a half (0.5) — the half-penny symbol is present and interpreted as 0.5. The descriptive phrase about Dr Richards and payment by 'Mr Glubb' is largely legible; minor letter ambiguity in surnames but context supports reading.
rows.english_description: Increment (additional rent) for the room of Dr Richards for Michaelmas / summer 1807, paid by Mr Glubb and previously not accounted for (arrears).
rows.english_description_with_amount: Increment for the room of Dr Richards for Michaelmas/summer 1807, paid by Mr Glubb and previously not accounted for, amounting to £88 3s 6½d.

rows.notes: Final boxed total at lower right reads '1307.14.3 3/4' — pounds, shillings, pence and three-quarter penny fraction (0.75) clearly visible. Column alignment and digits are clear; fraction '3/4' legible.
